options: Record as 100% business asset (vehicle) with taxable benefit tracking for personal commuting
Split acquisition into business and personal portions based on expected use
ambiguities.clarification_question: Will the vehicle be recorded as a 100% business asset (with taxable benefits tracked for personal use), or should the acquisition be split between business and personal portions based on expected usage?
ambiguities.why_entry_differs: 100% business recording debits full cost to Vehicle asset; split allocation debits a portion to Shareholder Loan or other personal account. This affects both the initial entry and ongoing depreciation calculations.
ambiguities.why_not_resolved: The description states 'executive's commuting and business use' indicating mixed use, but does not specify the accounting treatment election. Canadian tax rules allow either approach - corporations commonly record as 100% business asset with standby charges, but some allocate at acquisition. The text does not indicate which method Target has chosen.

ambiguities.resolution: 'Sedan' explicitly stated in transaction text - this is a passenger vehicle subject to CCA Class 10 or 10.1 depending on cost threshold

Transaction explicitly states '10% non-recoverable sales tax' - this indicates the combined rate and non-recoverability, providing sufficient information for the entry. In Ontario, vehicle PST (at time of many transactions) was non-recoverable while HST has recoverable and non-recoverable portions, but the explicit 10% non-recoverable direction is clear.
'3-month dealer payment plan' with $52,800 total payable is stated - this is short-term financing creating a liability for the full financed amount
No interest mentioned and total financed ($52,800) equals the invoiced amount including tax - indicates zero-interest or interest-free dealer financing, no separate interest component to record
